differences.title: 1. PRIMARY USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Captures all changes to state as a sequence of events.
differences.rightDesc: Continuously moves data between applications and systems.

differences.title: 2. DATA STORAGE
differences.leftDesc: Stores events permanently, forming the definitive history.
differences.rightDesc: Messages are typically consumed and deleted.

differences.title: 3. CONSUMPTION MODEL
differences.leftDesc: Rebuilds current state by replaying events.
differences.rightDesc: Processes individual events as they arrive.

differences.title: 4. READ OPERATIONS
differences.leftDesc: Reads are served from optimized, separate projections.
differences.rightDesc: Reads directly from the stream as it flows.

differences.title: 5. COMPLEXITY
differences.leftDesc: Requires careful design of events and projections.
differences.rightDesc: Focuses on message reliability and throughput.

differences.title: 6. USE FOR ANALYTICS
differences.leftDesc: Provides a complete, auditable trail of all actions.
differences.rightDesc: Enables real-time analytics dashboards and alerts.

differences.title: 7. ERROR HANDLING
differences.leftDesc: Event handlers must be robust against replays.
differences.rightDesc: May deliver events multiple times, requiring deduplication.
